title: Soft Language Prompts for Language Transfer

authors.label: Authors
authors.text: Vykopal Ivan, Bc. , DCGM (FIT) Ostermann Simon Simko Marian, doc. Ing., Ph.D. , DCGM (FIT)

abstract.label: Abstract
abstract.text: Cross-lingual knowledge transfer, especially between high- and low-resource languages, remains challenging in natural language processing (NLP). This study offers insights for improving cross-lingual NLP applications through the combination of parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods. We systematically explore strategies for enhancing cross-lingual transfer through the incorporation of language-specific and task-specific adapters and soft prompts. We present a detailed investigation of various combinations of these methods, exploring their efficiency across 16 languages, focusing on 10 mid- and low-resource languages. We further present to our knowledge the first use of soft prompts for language transfer, a technique we call soft language prompts. Our findings demonstrate that in contrast to claims of previous work, a combination of language and task adapters does not always work best; instead, combining a soft language prompt with a task adapter outperforms most configurations in many cases.

keywords.label: Keywords
keywords.text: cross-lingual transfer, multilinguality, less-resourced languages, language representations
